title: Zoro Takes Up His Swords
description: On pages 17–19, this part centers on Zoro’s endurance, Helmeppo’s cruelty, and Luffy’s decision to recruit the captured swordsman.
scene: In Shells Town, Luffy, Coby, and Zoro are drawn into the cruelty of Morgan’s rule and Helmeppo’s privilege.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Who's gonna be King of the Pirates!!!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I didn't know, so I brought all three...

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: If you fight the navy with me here and now, in the eyes of the government, you'll be one of the bad guys!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: OF COURSE, YOU COULD OBEY THE LAW... AND LET THEM KILL YOU!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: BUT I'M NOT READY TO DIE WITHOUT A FIGHT! ALL RIGHT!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: This is what I came up with. Originally his name wasn't Morgan, it was "Chop." So his full title was "Naval Captain chop" or "Sailor Chop!" What a great name! But even I didn't have the nerve to use this name. [Editor's Note:

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: I can't show them all here, but I actually made two or three more versions of Captain Morgan before I settled on the one used in the manga. I redesigned him to look cooler because a certain editor told me he looked lame. I couldn't argue with that, so I changed his look.

summary: The Shells Town conflict uses Zoro’s imprisonment and Morgan’s rule to test what kind of people Luffy chooses to trust. The section moves Zoro closer to becoming Luffy’s first crewmate.
